questions.meaning: Words that sound the same but have different meanings

questions.sample_sentence: My cousin Vishwak and I wandered through the stalls at the Marina Beach fair, trying every snack we could find.
I wondered why my Dhrupad teacher changed the time of my music class without informing me earlier.
During our family vacation to the hills, we drove through beautiful green vales covered with wildflowers.

questions.meaning: a country in the United Kingdom
questions.sample_sentence: We learnt in geography class at Sishya that Wales has its own language and many ancient castles.
A massive crowd gathered at Chepauk Stadium to cheer for CSK during the IPL final match.
The rooster crowed so loudly at my grandmother's house that it woke up even my cousin Prahlad.
My father's course is called Way2top because it shows students the way to a successful career.
When my mother makes paneer at home, she saves the whey to use it for kneading dough for chapatis.
My heart was filled with joy when Patri Satish Kumar appreciated my mridangam playing at a small gathering.

questions.meaning: male deer over five years old
questions.sample_sentence: We saw a majestic hart with enormous antlers at the wildlife sanctuary during our family vacation.
Our English teacher at Sishya showed us pictures of daffodils before we started studying the poem by Wordsworth.
I always draw a neat margin on the left side of my notebook before starting to write my class notes.
Nandi the dog is so sprightly that he runs around the entire garden without stopping even for a moment.
We were all in a jocund mood after CSK won the IPL match by a huge margin of runs.
I sat in a pensive mood after my Dhrupad class, thinking about the raga I had just learnt.
I enjoy the solitude of early mornings when I can practise my mridangam without any disturbance at home.
My friend Yohann jumped with glee when he won his first tennis tournament at the junior level.
A host of delivery riders from Swiggy can be seen zipping through the streets of Chennai every evening.

questions.meaning: galaxy visible as band of stars
questions.sample_sentence: On our family vacation to a hill station, we could see the milky way clearly in the dark night sky.
My cousin Vishwak and I oft argue about whether cricket or football is the better sport to play.
The vacant plot near Tidel Park in Chennai has now been turned into a parking area for office workers.
The golden dosa served by Sastha Catering Services at our family function looked absolutely delicious.
The sparkling waves at Marina Beach looked magical under the bright afternoon sun during our weekend visit.
The continuous rain in Chennai during December flooded many roads near Greams Road and made travel difficult.
The never-ending queue at the biryani stall during the school food festival tested everyone's patience that day.
My Dhrupad teacher says that music is an inward journey that helps us connect with our deeper self.
I felt lonely when my cousin Prahlad left Chennai after spending the entire summer vacation with us.
The school flags were fluttering in the wind during the sports day celebration at Sishya.
I could see the lights of the buildings near Tidel Park twinkle from our terrace on clear nights.
I took a quick glance at the scoreboard during the IPL match and saw that CSK needed only ten runs to win.
We were all in a gay mood on the last day of school before the summer holidays began.
After eating a huge plate of idiappam and curry, I lay down on the couch and fell asleep instantly.
Playing the mridangam along with my Dhrupad teacher's singing is a moment of pure bliss for me.
It gives me great pleasure to watch Suvasana and Yohann compete with each other in every board game we play.
My father always says that true wealth is the knowledge and skills we gain, not just money in the bank.
